findings.description: The signature/form fields section layout is significantly different. In the PDF, the DATE, SIGNATURE, PRINTED NAME, TITLE, FACILITY ADDRESS, FACILITY CITY & ZIP, FACILITY PHONE, and EMAIL fields are arranged in a multi-column layout (DATE on left, SIGNATURE on right, other fields centered). In the HTML, all fields are rendered in a single-column stacked layout.
findings.evidence: PDF shows DATE and SIGNATURE side-by-side on the same line with other fields in a structured two-column arrangement. HTML renders all form fields sequentially in a single column.

findings.description: The NC Waste Management / Environmental Quality logo appears as a mostly blacked-out rectangle in the HTML version, losing the distinctive NC logo design visible in the PDF.
findings.evidence: PDF shows a clear NC logo with 'Waste Management ENVIRONMENTAL QUALITY' text beneath. HTML shows a black rectangle with the text below it.

findings.description: The Initials column in the checklist table appears empty in both versions, but the table structure is preserved with correct row counts and column alignment.
findings.evidence: Both PDF and HTML show the same table structure with Initials column and Minimum Management Practices column.

findings.description: The main title 'DRY-CLEANING SOLVENT CLEANUP ACT (DSCA)' and subtitle formatting differ slightly. The PDF shows them as centered bold text, while the HTML renders them left-aligned with a different visual hierarchy.
findings.evidence: PDF: centered bold titles. HTML: left-aligned titles with horizontal rule separators instead of centered formatting.

findings.description: The subtitle 'Voluntary Self-inspection Checklist for Petroleum and Alternative-solvent Dry Cleaners' is rendered as h2 inside a header element, but the PDF treats it as a subtitle directly under the main title, not a separate section heading. This is an acceptable stylistic choice but worth noting.
findings.evidence:
HTML:

Voluntary Self-inspection Checklist for Petroleum and Alternative-solvent Dry Cleaners

findings.description: The HTML contains nested <main> elements, which results in the entire content structure being duplicated in terms of semantic structure. While the text content itself is not visually duplicated, the double <main> nesting is a structural error.

The URL for the MMPs rule is split across two anchor tags in the HTML, whereas in the PDF it is a single hyperlink. Both point to the same destination. This is a minor formatting difference, not fabrication.
findings.evidence: HTML has two separate <a> tags for the same URL: one with text 'https://deq.nc.gov/about/divisions/waste-' and another with 'management/superfund-section/special-remediation-branch/compliance-unit#regulations'

findings.description: The alt text for the NC DEQ logo image reads 'North Carolina Department of Environmental Quality logo', which is a reasonable description. The PDF shows an 'NC' logo with 'Waste Management / ENVIRONMENTAL QUALITY' text beneath it. The alt text is acceptable though slightly elaborated.
findings.evidence: HTML: alt='North Carolina Department of Environmental Quality logo'. PDF shows NC logo with 'Waste Management ENVIRONMENTAL QUALITY' label.
